blocks.content: The woman watched closely the red light emanating from Shirone's body.
If it vibrated particles by releasing high energy, it could destroy any object.

blocks.speaker: The woman
blocks.content: Shirone, stop this. That magic is too destructive. It doesn't suit your nature.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I don't want to fight either. But you intend to keep me here. I can't allow that. I must return.

blocks.speaker: The woman
blocks.content: Do you think you can win against me?
Shirone shook his head.
He was being honest. Even as a being of the same dimension, she claimed to be a God with formidable power. At the very least, she was stronger than Arcane.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Who was this woman? And where was this place?
Shirone stopped thinking.
At least, this wasn't where he belonged.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Tell me how to return to my original world.

blocks.speaker: The woman
blocks.content: Even if I told you, it's impossible. It would only lead you to death again.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I still want to try.
The woman wasn't afraid of Shirone's magic. But the laser's power wasn't something to dismiss lightly. She couldn't let this world be destroyed.

blocks.speaker: The woman
blocks.content: Fine. Have it your way. But promise me this. If you fail to return and come back, then follow my will.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: How do I return?

blocks.speaker: The woman
blocks.content: I can't concede any further. Will you still do it?
No matter how daunting the obstacle, Shirone's resolve was unchanged.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Fine. But I'll never give up.

blocks.speaker: The woman
blocks.content: Your life is yours. Use it as you wish.
The woman created a glass orb before Shirone.
Inside, black darkness swirled.

blocks.speaker: The woman
blocks.content: Shirone. To return to your original world, you must reverse the process that brought you here.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I see.

It won't be easy. Returning from pure spirit to human. Humans are aggregates of all kinds of unpleasant elements. If you can't accept this, you'll end up as neither human nor anything else—just a strange aberration.

blocks.speaker: The woman
blocks.content: The darkness is a spot piercing through spacetime. Commonly called a black hole. You can return to your original world through it.
The orb vanished, and a massive darkness appeared before Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What must I do?

blocks.speaker: The woman
blocks.content: It's simple. Enter. It's the spot connected to your body.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Thank you. I won't forget your kindness.
Shirone meant it. Though hijacked, without her, he'd have had no chance to return.

blocks.speaker: The woman
blocks.content: Don't thank me prematurely. I don't believe you'll succeed. If you want to give up, just walk back to me.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Would that happen? If he died, he died—he wouldn't return. But since he hadn't experienced it, Shirone couldn't say for sure.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Then I'll go.
The woman half-closed her eyes sleepily.

blocks.speaker: The woman
blocks.content: Have a safe journey.
Shirone took his first step into the orb.
It felt similar to when he first entered the spot.
As expected, he couldn't find an exit. No matter how much he walked, nothing changed.
Turning back in surprise, Shirone saw the woman still watching him sleepily.
Time wasn't passing. He was trapped at the event horizon, where even light couldn't escape.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I can't escape. It's absolutely impossible to leave this place.
Shirone finally understood the woman's words.
In a place where even time couldn't escape, there was nothing he could do.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: His intelligence made him face reality quickly.
Shirone turned back in despair.
Better to live among people than be trapped in eternal darkness.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wait. Maybe……
Shirone suddenly stopped.
At the event horizon, time was eternally trapped. But he had the power to generate time: photon magic.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Sub-light, near-light, light, superluminal.
Photon mages classified speed into four types.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: By Shirone's calculations, surpassing the event horizon required speed faster than light—superluminal.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Superluminal…… Is it possible?
Shirone's current level was barely sub-light.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: With divine particles, near-light might be possible, but that was still far from superluminal.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Let me think.
Now that he had a method, time wasn't an issue.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: After long contemplation, his eyes changed.
It might be possible.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Without a physical body, achieving superluminal speed was feasible.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Immortal Function.
Even light speed couldn't traverse the vast universe. But infinite expansion was instantaneous.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Perhaps the Immortal Function was the key to reaching superluminal speed.
Either way, returning was the priority.
Shirone took a deep breath and activated photon magic while simultaneously opening the Immortal Function.
The effect was immediate.
Tremors from accelerating to sub-light, near-light, and light speed shook the Spirit Zone to the brink of collapse.
Ugh……!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yet he couldn't reach superluminal.
Without comprehending speed faster than time, magic through cognition was impossible.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The only option was fully opening the Immortal Function.
He might vanish again into the unknown, but without hesitation, he expanded his consciousness to the whole.
Shirone's form burst apart, dissolving into particles.
Finally, something faster than light crossed the event horizon and raced toward the light.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's working!
Shirone still retained consciousness.
Even with the Immortal Function fully open, no particle scattered inside the black hole.
Far ahead, the exit awaited—where his body was.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm going! Finally going!
Suddenly, his speed began to drop.
Unintelligible particles clung to Shirone's particles.
Combining, they created meaning, acting like voices that disoriented his mind.
-Feed. Feed. Feed. Feed. Feed. Feed. Feed. Feed.
-Breed. Breed. Breed. Breed. Breed. Breed. Breed. Breed.
As Shirone grew heavier, the exit receded.
The particles intertwined complexly, evolving simple meanings into higher ones.
-Ah, to be born. The lowest existence.
-I don't want to die. A mortal's life.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone realized this was what the woman meant by becoming human.
A being embracing both desire and fear, cast into the world.
Perhaps all humans heard these voices in the womb.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I won't lose! I will return!
-To where? There's nowhere to welcome you.
To my world! Where my friends await!
-They'll kill you. Interfere with feeding and breeding. What awaits you is the clash of desires, painful struggle, and inevitable death.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ahhh! I hear nothing! No words can stop me!
Shirone screamed desperately.
-Feed. Feed. Feed. Feed. Feed. Feed. Feed. Feed.
-Breed. Breed. Breed. Breed. Breed. Breed. Breed. Breed.
On the seventh day since Shirone's death.
The student council was still protesting.
Specifically, the radicals following Amira.
In opposition, conservative students blocked the entrance, barring access.
Moderates also split into factions, doubling the protesters.
Most of the school had gathered in front of the building, protesting day and night.
The friends guarding Shirone were on the verge of nervous breakdowns.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Hold Shirone's funeral!

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hold it! Hold it!

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Alpheas must face the Magic Association's judgment!

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Face it! Face it!
Seriel covered her ears.
With the window closed, the noise wasn't loud, but she couldn't take it anymore.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Aren't they going too far? Why are they so obsessed with Shirone?
Amy, who had been dozing with her face buried, lifted her head at Seriel's words.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: They have their reasons. Some probably never liked Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Is it Shirone's fault for being exceptional? They're just narrow-minded.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: That's how people are. For every person who acknowledges Shirone, there's one who rejects him.
At the table, Iruki, who had been lying face down, spoke.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Did you have an epiphany? Just yesterday, you said you'd chase them down and beat them up.
Amy didn't even have the energy to get angry.
Iruki was probably the same. After a week of stress, they couldn't be bothered to lift a finger.
Amy rested her chin on the bed.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Seeing things now, I get it. At least some are fighting for Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: But our side is mostly lower or upper class. The protesters are mostly graduating class. If they really charge, we can't stop them.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: We're fine for now. We have the faculty. The headmaster, Siena, and Ethela are on our side.

blocks.speaker: Neid
blocks.content: You don't know, Senior. They've been acting suspicious since yesterday.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Huh? How?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Some are missing. Especially Amira and Fermi. At least one should stay to agitate.
Amy flared up.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hey! You use honorifics with Seriel but keep dropping them with me!
Iruki looked away, annoyed.
There was no particular reason.
If Shirone's girlfriend was his friend, then so was she.
The infirmary door opened, and Alpheas entered with Siena and Ethela.
The teachers' faces were haggard from the student council's persistence.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: You've worked hard. How is Shirone?

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: As you can see…… unchanged.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: I see. It can't be helped.
Even Alpheas was starting to doubt.
A week had passed.
If some absolute being claimed Shirone would return, he'd wait a year or even ten.
But no one could prove Shirone would come back.
It was a battle against time.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: I'll cast a spell on Shirone.
Amy and Seriel made way.
Alpheas placed his hand on Shirone's chest and cast a slow spell.
It was preservation without harming the body.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Finished.
Alpheas stepped back.
Shirone would last another day.
But how much longer?
The teachers were silently observing now, but complaints were inevitable.
Siena stared at Shirone, then stroked his head.
The more she thought, the more unfair and bitter it felt.
A promising mage, cut down before blooming.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: I told you to stop.
Shirone overcame Overflow.
But the cost was frequent contact with the Immortal Function.

blocks.speaker: Ethela
blocks.content: Back then, I vowed to take full responsibility.
She didn't want to criticize now.

blocks.speaker: Ethela
blocks.content: If Shirone didn't make it, she'd spend her life mourning his spirit.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Teacher! It's bad!
Amy and Neid also looked out the window, unable to hide their anger.
Siena walked to the window.
Amira and Fermi, absent yesterday, were now waving documents at the infirmary.

blocks.speaker: Amira
blocks.content: On behalf of Alpheas Magic School students, we submit indictments against all faculty! Hand over Headmaster Alpheas!
Siena's head throbbed.
Her worst fears had come true.
The student council had played their strongest card.
They weren't just targeting Alpheas—they were demanding punishment for all teachers.
Once the indictments were submitted, the situation would spiral out of control.
With the students' families involved, the school would collapse beyond recovery.
